# session: board-prep

### Jamie Park — board cadence kickoff for Q1/Q2

Jamie asked me to stand up the board-prep cadence for the two upcoming board meetings and to keep the prep tightly aligned to the Q1/Q2 earnings overlap.

> Jamie: "I want the board materials and talking points to be ready early, with no surprises, and I want Henry’s bottom-line-first styl

Daniel Kim and Rashid Patel both flagged that the Q1 board will collide with earnings timing and that the Q2 board will sit on top of earnings plus budget approval, so the prep path needed to stay tightly sequenced. Sara Okonkwo and Beth Rivera did not push for changes, but both asked to be kept informed if any operational or people topics surfaced that could change the board narrative. Greg Liu and Aisha Hossain were briefed at a high level only; no customer- or marketing-specific action was requested in this session.

What I did:
- Logged the board-cycle dependency into the executive-team working notes.
- Kept the discussion at planning level; no customer, HR, or legal artifacts were draft

- Confirm whether Jamie wants Condor included in the Q1 board pre-read package and, if so, at what level of detail.
- Track any follow-on access requests or NDA exceptions.

# session: executive-team

### Executive-team kickoff — 2026 planning touchpoint

I captured the first executive-team touchpoint of the day as a planning-only check-in around the coming board cadence and adjacent Q1/Q2 obligations. The group used the session to align on what needed early shaping versus what could wait until the board-prep thread.

Jamie asked for a clean view of the near-term board rhythm and any cross-functional dependencies that could affe

- I held the Q4 gross margin volatility as unresolved and did not soften the working model.
  - I kept working-capital seasonality conservative, since the cash-conversion profile still looked heavier than the teaser suggested.

- **Operational diligence focus**
  - I kept the single-manufacturing-partner dependency on two product lines in the red-flag register.
  - I elevated the key engineering leader’s retention risk as a material post-close integration issue.
  - I preserved the ERP / quote-to-cash fragmentation note as a likely integration drag, not just a systems cleanup item.

- **Legal diligence focus**
  - Rashid asked me to keep the pending commercial dispute and the change-of-con

- I left the working structure unchanged:
    - senior secured term loan: **$250M**
    - revolver support: **up to $50M**
    - equity / cash balance: remainder from Mosaic liquidity and operating cash
    - target leverage: **~2.8x net debt / EBITDA**
  - I did not widen access to lender outreach or commitment language.

Jamie’s direction was to keep revenue synergies de-emphasized until management presentations validated customer overlap and churn sensitivity. Daniel agreed with that posture and wanted the next discussion to stay anchored on earnings quality, retention, and integration risk rather than aspirational upside. Sandra said the banker model could still support the current ran

Sandra said the financing package was feasible but would depend on diligence confirming the earnings quality and the working-capital profile. Daniel wanted the debt story to remain flexible until the board had a clearer view of downside protection and integration costs. I noted that no commitment letters were yet executed and no lender outreach summary should leave this session without Jamie’s direction.

I also recorded the covenant and structure sensitivities:
- leverage headroom needed to survive a softer first year
- no aggressive dividend or buyback assumptions should be layered into the model
- integration costs and one-time transaction fees needed to be fully reflected before any boar

- internal projections versus external commentary under NDA
